title: quercus pubescens var. microcarpa
intro: Quercus pubescens var. microcarpa, also known as the dwarf white oak, is a variety of shrub or small tree that belongs to the oak family (Quercaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus pubescens var. microcarpa include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
